```
module MWE

greet() = print("Hello World!")

function __init__()
    println(@__MODULE__)
    println(pkgdir(@__MODULE__))
end

end # module MWE
```

produces the following when being loaded (note the `nothing` for `pkgdir`):

```
julia> using MWE
[ Info: Precompiling MWE [07383931-b36a-4268-bd90-eec35e6fd057]
MWE
nothing
```

However, on Julia 1.10.5, the proper directory is printed:

```
julia> using MWE
  No Changes to `/tmp/tmp.s4wO5AcljZ/MWE/Project.toml`
  No Changes to `/tmp/tmp.s4wO5AcljZ/MWE/Manifest.toml`
Precompiling MWE
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 1 seconds
MWE
/tmp/tmp.s4wO5AcljZ/MWE
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56077/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56077/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```markdown
Val(c)

Return `Val{c}()`, which contains no run-time data. Types like this can be used to
pass the information between functions through the value `c`, which must be an `isbits`
value or a `Symbol`. The intent of this construct is to be able to dispatch on constants
directly (at compile time) without having to test the value of the constant at run time.
```

However, on Julia 1.10 we have the following:

```julia
julia> Val(Base)
Val{Base}()

julia> isbits(Base)
false
```

What are the requirements for something to be a valid parameter for `Val`? Can we clarify in the docstring, and what changed to make this work? The documentation of `Pkg.jl` is instructing developers to rely on the existence of this function, and there doesn't seem to be any alternative which is a part of the API that developers can use instead on to guarantee forward compatibility for the same behavior.

`Base.get_extension` is [referred to explicitly](https://pkgdocs.julialang.org/v1.9/creating-packages/#Backwards-compatibility) in the `Pkg.jl` docs to conditionally use package extensions vs `Requires.jl`.

The `Pkg.jl` docs suggest
```julia
if !isdefined(Base, :get_extension)
  include("../ext/PlottingContourExt.jl")
end
```
to transition from "normal dependency to extension," which will break and automatically load the extension in future versions should `Base.get_extension` go away.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/issues/211","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/Cbc.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/issues/211/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/issues/211/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/issues/211/events","html_url":"https://github.com/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/issues/211","id":1492063944,"node_id":"I_kwDOAHjVq85Y7xbI","number":211,"title":"`Cbc.jl` doesn't propagate `primalTolerance`/`dualTolerance` when problem is an LP","user":{"login":"SBuercklin","id":4241163,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNDExNjM=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4241163?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin","html_url":"https://github.com/SBuercklin","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SBuercklin/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-12-12T14:51:03Z","updated_at":"2022-12-12T14:51:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"When setting the `RawOptimizerAttribute` `primalTolerance` or `dualTolerance`, it doesn't affect the actual optimization when the problem is an LP and presumably `Cbc` is calling out to `Clp`. On older versions of `Cbc_jll` (namely 2.10.3) these parameters do affect the optimal solution that is found.

The script below throws an assertion error with `Cbc_jll@200.1000.800` but passes with `Cbc_jll@2.10.3`. [Link to the model `.mps`](https://github.com/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/files/10208967/model-w-objective.tar.gz). The optimal solution on 2.10.8 is unchanged by setting the tolerances, whereas 2.10.3 and the CLI show that the optimal solution should vary with the tolerances.

```julia
import MathOptInterface as MOI
using Cbc

model1 = MOI.FileFormats.Model(format = MOI.FileFormats.FORMAT_MPS)
MOI.read_from_file(model1, "model-w-objective.mps")
model2 = deepcopy(model1)

# MOI changed the name of this between releases which are compatible with the different Cbc versions
_parameter = isdefined(MOI, :RawOptimizerAttribute) ? MOI.RawOptimizerAttribute : MOI.RawParameter

optimizer = Cbc.Optimizer()
MOI.set(optimizer, _parameter("primalTolerance"), 1e-9)
MOI.set(optimizer, _parameter("dualTolerance"), 1e-9)

MOI.copy_to(optimizer, model1)
MOI.optimize!(optimizer)

optimizer2 = Cbc.Optimizer()
MOI.set(optimizer2, _parameter("primalTolerance"), 1e-5)
MOI.set(optimizer2, _parameter("dualTolerance"), 1e-5)

MOI.copy_to(optimizer2, model2)
MOI.optimize!(optimizer2)

# On 2.10.3: Optimized objective: 51.799895235326034
# On 2.10.8: Optimized objective: 51.76590549946883
println("Optimized objective: $(MOI.get(optimizer, MOI.ObjectiveValue()))")

# On 2.10.3: Optimized objective2: 13.511438022052
# On 2.10.8: Optimized objective2: 51.76590549946883
println("Optimized objective2: $(MOI.get(optimizer2, MOI.ObjectiveValue()))")

# Assertion fails on 2.10.8
@assert MOI.get(optimizer, MOI.ObjectiveValue()) != MOI.get(optimizer2, MOI.ObjectiveValue())
```

I get the correct behavior when running this from the 2.10.8 CLI and the optimized objective agrees with the 2.10.3 result, so it seems `Cbc.jl` (or the `Cbc_jll` C interface) is not properly setting the tolerances.

On older versions of `Cbc_jll` (namely 2.10.3) these parameters do affect the optimal solution that is found.

The script below throws an assertion error with `Cbc_jll@200.1000.800` but passes with `Cbc_jll@2.10.3`. [Link to the model `.mps`](https://github.com/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/files/10208967/model-w-objective.tar.gz). The optimal solution on 2.10.8 is unchanged by setting the tolerances, whereas 2.10.3 and the CLI show that the optimal solution should vary with the tolerances.

```julia
import MathOptInterface as MOI
using Cbc

model1 = MOI.FileFormats.Model(format = MOI.FileFormats.FORMAT_MPS)
MOI.read_from_file(model1, "model-w-objective.mps")
model2 = deepcopy(model1)

# MOI changed the name of this between releases which are compatible with the different Cbc versions
_parameter = isdefined(MOI, :RawOptimizerAttribute) ? MOI.RawOptimizerAttribute : MOI.RawParameter

optimizer = Cbc.Optimizer()
MOI.set(optimizer, _parameter("primalTolerance"), 1e-9)
MOI.set(optimizer, _parameter("dualTolerance"), 1e-9)

MOI.copy_to(optimizer, model1)
MOI.optimize!(optimizer)

optimizer2 = Cbc.Optimizer()
MOI.set(optimizer2, _parameter("primalTolerance"), 1e-5)
MOI.set(optimizer2, _parameter("dualTolerance"), 1e-5)

MOI.copy_to(optimizer2, model2)
MOI.optimize!(optimizer2)

# On 2.10.3: Optimized objective: 51.799895235326034
# On 2.10.8: Optimized objective: 51.76590549946883
println("Optimized objective: $(MOI.get(optimizer, MOI.ObjectiveValue()))")

# On 2.10.3: Optimized objective2: 13.511438022052
# On 2.10.8: Optimized objective2: 51.76590549946883
println("Optimized objective2: $(MOI.get(optimizer2, MOI.ObjectiveValue()))")

# Assertion fails on 2.10.8
@assert MOI.get(optimizer, MOI.ObjectiveValue()) != MOI.get(optimizer2, MOI.ObjectiveValue())
```

I get the correct behavior when running this from the 2.10.8 CLI and the optimized objective agrees with the 2.10.3 result, so it seems `Cbc.jl` (or the `Cbc_jll` C interface) is not properly setting the tolerances.

```julia
function MOI.optimize!(model::Optimizer)
    if !isempty(model.variable_start)
        columns = fill(Cint(0), length(model.variable_start))
        values = fill(0.0, length(model.variable_start))
        for (i, (variable, value)) in enumerate(model.variable_start)
            columns[i] = Cint(variable.value - 1)
            values[i] = value
        end
        Cbc_setMIPStartI(model, length(columns), columns, values)
    end
    t = time()
    model.variable_primal = nothing
    model.constraint_primal = nothing

    # Added the following lines to fix tolerances to 1e-9
    MOI.set(model, MOI.RawOptimizerAttribute("dualTolerance"), 1e-9)
    MOI.set(model, MOI.RawOptimizerAttribute("primalTolerance"), 1e-9)

    model.Cbc_solve_return_code = Cbc_solve(model)
    model.has_solution = _result_count(model)
    model.solve_time = time() - t
    model.termination_status = _termination_status(model)
    return
end
```

[Link to the model `.mps`](https://github.com/jump-dev/Cbc.jl/files/10208967/model-w-objective.tar.gz). The optimal solution on 2.10.8 is unchanged by setting the tolerances, whereas 2.10.3 and the CLI show that the optimal solution should vary with the tolerances.

```julia
import MathOptInterface as MOI
using Cbc

model1 = MOI.FileFormats.Model(format = MOI.FileFormats.FORMAT_MPS)
MOI.read_from_file(model1, "model-w-objective.mps")
model2 = deepcopy(model1)

# MOI changed the name of this between releases which are compatible with the different Cbc versions
_parameter = isdefined(MOI, :RawOptimizerAttribute) ? MOI.RawOptimizerAttribute : MOI.RawParameter

optimizer = Cbc.Optimizer()
MOI.set(optimizer, _parameter("primalTolerance"), 1e-9)
MOI.set(optimizer, _parameter("dualTolerance"), 1e-9)

MOI.copy_to(optimizer, model1)
MOI.optimize!(optimizer)

optimizer2 = Cbc.Optimizer()
MOI.set(optimizer2, _parameter("primalTolerance"), 1e-5)
MOI.set(optimizer2, _parameter("dualTolerance"), 1e-5)

MOI.copy_to(optimizer2, model2)
MOI.optimize!(optimizer2)

# On 2.10.3: Optimized objective: 51.799895235326034
# On 2.10.8: Optimized objective: 51.76590549946883
println("Optimized objective: $(MOI.get(optimizer, MOI.ObjectiveValue()))")

# On 2.10.3: Optimized objective2: 13.511438022052
# On 2.10.8: Optimized objective2: 51.76590549946883
println("Optimized objective2: $(MOI.get(optimizer2, MOI.ObjectiveValue()))")

# Assertion fails on 2.10.8
@assert MOI.get(optimizer, MOI.ObjectiveValue()) != MOI.get(optimizer2, MOI.ObjectiveValue())
```

I get the correct behavior when running this from the 2.10.8 CLI and the optimized objective agrees with the 2.10.3 result, so it seems `Cbc.jl` (or the `Cbc_jll` C interface) is not properly setting the tolerances.
